ast.name: innerHTML
ast.source: The file or folder to load snippets from. The file or folder must be within your vault, and not within a hidden folder (such as .obsidian/).
ast.target: The file or folder to load snippets from. The file or folder must be within your vault, and not within a hidden folder (such as .obsidian/).

ast.name: innerHTML
ast.source: e.g. \\dot{x}^{2} + \\dot{y}^{2} will display as \u1E8B\xB2 + \u1E8F\xB2, and \\sqrt{ 1-\\beta^{2} } will display as \u221A{ 1-\u03B2\xB2 }.
ast.target: e.g. \\dot{x}^{2} + \\dot{y}^{2} will display as \u1E8B\xB2 + \u1E8F\xB2, and \\sqrt{ 1-\\beta^{2} } will display as \u221A{ 1-\u03B2\xB2 }.

ast.name: innerHTML
ast.source: The file or folder to load snippet variables from. The file or folder must be within your vault, and not within a hidden folder (such as .obsidian/).
ast.target: The file or folder to load snippet variables from. The file or folder must be within your vault, and not within a hidden folder (such as .obsidian/).

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Enter snippets here.  Remember to add a comma after each snippet, and escape all backslashes with an extra \. Lines starting with "//" will be treated as comments and ignored.
ast.target: Enter snippets here.  Remember to add a comma after each snippet, and escape all backslashes with an extra \. Lines starting with "//" will be treated as comments and ignored.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Whether to load snippets from a specified file, or from all files within a folder (instead of from the plugin settings).
ast.target: Whether to load snippets from a specified file, or from all files within a folder (instead of from the plugin settings).

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: When the cursor is adjacent to a bracket, highlight the matching bracket.
ast.target: When the cursor is adjacent to a bracket, highlight the matching bracket.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Where to display the popup preview relative to the equation source.
ast.target: Where to display the popup preview relative to the equation source.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Whether to highlight the area within the nearest pair of brackets around the cursor in the popup preview.
ast.target: Whether to highlight the area within the nearest pair of brackets around the cursor in the popup preview.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: The fraction symbol to use in the replacement. e.g. \frac, \dfrac, \tfrac
ast.target: The fraction symbol to use in the replacement. e.g. \frac, \dfrac, \tfrac

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: A list of environments to exclude auto-fraction from running in. For example, to exclude auto-fraction from running while inside an exponent, such as e^{...}, use  ["^{", "}"]
ast.target: A list of environments to exclude auto-fraction from running in. For example, to exclude auto-fraction from running while inside an exponent, such as e^{...}, use  ["^{", "}"]

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: A list of characters that denote the start/end of a fraction. e.g. if + is included in the list, "a+b/c" will expand to "a+\frac{b}{c}". If + is not in the list, it will expand to "\frac{a+b}{c}".
ast.target: A list of characters that denote the start/end of a fraction. e.g. if + is included in the list, "a+b/c" will expand to "a+\frac{b}{c}". If + is not in the list, it will expand to "\frac{a+b}{c}".

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: A list of environment names to run the matrix shortcuts in, separated by commas.
ast.target: A list of environment names to run the matrix shortcuts in, separated by commas.

ast.name: setDesc

ast.target: A list of closing brackets for tabout, separated by commas.

ast.target: Whether to automatically enlarge brackets containing e.g. sum, int, frac.

ast.target: A list of symbols that should trigger auto-enlarge brackets, separated by commas.

ast.target: Assign snippet variables that can be used as shortcuts when writing snippets.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Whether to load snippet variables from a specified file, or from all files within a folder (instead of from the plugin settings).
ast.target: Whether to load snippet variables from a specified file, or from all files within a folder (instead of from the plugin settings).

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Symbols that will be treated as word delimiters, for use with the "w" snippet option.
ast.target: Symbols that will be treated as word delimiters, for use with the "w" snippet option.

ast.name: setDesc

ast.target: Whether to remove trailing whitespaces when expanding snippets at the end of inline math blocks.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Whether to also remove the closing $ when you delete the opening $ symbol inside blank inline math.
ast.target: Whether to also remove the closing $ when you delete the opening $ symbol inside blank inline math.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Whether to suppress snippets triggering when an IME is active.
ast.target: Whether to suppress snippets triggering when an IME is active.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Whether a warning is shown on startup if `Don't trigger snippets when IME is active` is enabled. Disable that setting if you are aware of the IME limitations. Currently only ios and android touch/swipe keyboards like gboard have support for IME. See https://github.com/artisticat1/obsidian-latex-suite/blob/main/DOCS.md#IME-keyboards for more info.
ast.target: Whether a warning is shown on startup if `Don't trigger snippets when IME is active` is enabled. Disable that setting if you are aware of the IME limitations. Currently only ios and android touch/swipe keyboards like gboard have support for IME. See https://github.com/artisticat1/obsidian-latex-suite/blob/main/DOCS.md#IME-keyboards for more info.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Codeblock languages where the whole code block should be treated like a math block, separated by commas.
ast.target: Codeblock languages where the whole code block should be treated like a math block, separated by commas.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: Set the level of debug information to log about snippet expansion. Set to "info" or "verbose" to help identify issues with snippet syntax or why a snippet is not expanding. Verbose mode will log most information to the developer console on debug level.
ast.target: Set the level of debug information to log about snippet expansion. Set to "info" or "verbose" to help identify issues with snippet syntax or why a snippet is not expanding. Verbose mode will log most information to the developer console on debug level.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source: turn on/off vim keybindings. Note vim needs to be enabled in obsidian itself.
ast.target: turn on/off vim keybindings. Note vim needs to be enabled in obsidian itself.

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source:
maps the key to switch from visual mode to select mode.
				 Keymap must be a vim keymap and can't contain any spaces. Use empty string to disable this feature.
				  (select mode=insert keybindings)
ast.target:
maps the key to switch from visual mode to select mode.
				 Keymap must be a vim keymap and can't contain any spaces. Use empty string to disable this feature.
				  (select mode=insert keybindings)

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source:
maps the key to switch from select mode to visual mode. 
				 must be a vim keymap and can't contain any spaces. Example <C-g><C-A-i> = Ctrl-g + Ctrl-Alt-i.
				 Please check the vim keybinding first on another command like w before reporting it. Some keybindings like shift don't work due to the original vim plugin. Use empty string to disable this feature.
				  (select mode=insert keybindings)
ast.target:
maps the key to switch from select mode to visual mode. 
				 must be a vim keymap and can't contain any spaces. Example <C-g><C-A-i> = Ctrl-g + Ctrl-Alt-i.
				 Please check the vim keybinding first on another command like w before reporting it. Some keybindings like shift don't work due to the original vim plugin. Use empty string to disable this feature.
				  (select mode=insert keybindings)

ast.name: setDesc
ast.source:
maps the key to the action of inserting a new line below while appending \\\\ to the current line in a matrix environment.
				 Use empty string to disable this feature.
ast.target:
maps the key to the action of inserting a new line below while appending \\\\ to the current line in a matrix environment.
				 Use empty string to disable this feature.

ast.name: setText
ast.source: Make equations more readable by hiding LaTeX syntax and instead displaying it in a pretty format.
ast.target: Make equations more readable by hiding LaTeX syntax and instead displaying it in a pretty format.

ast.name: setText
ast.source: LaTeX beneath the cursor will be revealed.
ast.target: LaTeX beneath the cursor will be revealed.

ast.name: setText
ast.source: Disabled by default to not confuse new users. However, I recommend turning this on once you are comfortable with the plugin!
ast.target: Disabled by default to not confuse new users. However, I recommend turning this on once you are comfortable with the plugin!

ast.name: setText
ast.source: How long to delay the reveal of LaTeX for, in milliseconds, when the cursor moves over LaTeX. Defaults to 0 (LaTeX under the cursor is revealed immediately).
ast.target: How long to delay the reveal of LaTeX for, in milliseconds, when the cursor moves over LaTeX. Defaults to 0 (LaTeX under the cursor is revealed immediately).

ast.name: setText
ast.source: Can be set to a positive number, e.g. 300, to delay the reveal of LaTeX, making it much easier to navigate equations using arrow keys.
ast.target: Can be set to a positive number, e.g. 300, to delay the reveal of LaTeX, making it much easier to navigate equations using arrow keys.

ast.name: setText
ast.source: When inside an equation, show a popup preview window of the rendered math.
ast.target: When inside an equation, show a popup preview window of the rendered math.

ast.name: setText
ast.source: The popup preview will be shown for all inline math equations, as well as for block math equations in Source mode.
ast.target: The popup preview will be shown for all inline math equations, as well as for block math equations in Source mode.
